new_miner_training.timing: No less than 4 hours before new miner begins work; remaining 20 hours within 90 calendar days

required_topics.description: Tour of mine site, layout of facilities, identification of key personnel, reporting structure
Site-specific hazards including highwalls, mobile equipment, electrical, chemical, and dust exposures
Evacuation routes, alarm systems, rally points, fire response, injury reporting, emergency contacts

required_topics.topic: Health and safety aspects of assigned tasks
required_topics.description: Task-specific hazards, required PPE, safe work procedures, equipment-specific training
Right to report hazards, Part 46 protections, anti-discrimination provisions under Section 105(c), right to request MSHA inspection

required_topics.topic: Authority and responsibility of supervisors and miners' representatives
required_topics.description: Chain of command for safety, role of safety committee, miners' representative rights

required_topics.topic: Introduction to rules and procedures for reporting hazards
required_topics.description: Near-miss reporting, hazard identification forms, corrective action process
Location and use of self-rescue devices, dust masks, respirators as applicable

new_miner_training.notes: New miner must be accompanied by an experienced miner at all times until 24-hour requirement is met

experienced_miner_training.timing: Before starting work at the new mine
experienced_miner_training.condition: Miner has at least 12 months cumulative mining experience and has received new miner training
required_topics: Introduction to the work environment at the new mine
Recognition and avoidance of hazards present at the new mine
Emergency and evacuation procedures at the new mine
Health and safety aspects of tasks to be assigned
Authority and responsibility of supervisors and miners' representatives

annual_refresher_training.timing: Within 12 months of last refresher or initial training completion
content_guidance: Must include instruction on changes at the mine that could adversely affect safety or health
Should cover accident and injury trends from the prior year
Can be delivered in segments over the 12-month period
recommended_topics_for_cement_plants: LOTO refresher and recent incident review
Respirable crystalline silica exposure and controls
Fall protection and working at heights
Emergency response and evacuation drill review

task_training.required_hours: No minimum; must be sufficient to perform task safely
task_training.timing: Before miner performs the new task
triggers: Miner is assigned a new task not previously trained on
Change in equipment or procedures for an existing task
Observation reveals miner is not performing task safely
Miner is reassigned to an area with different hazards
requirements: Training must address equipment-specific hazards and safe operating procedures
Must include practice or demonstration under observation of competent person
Competency must be evaluated before miner performs task independently
common_cement_plant_tasks_requiring_trai: Confined space entry in silos or mills
LOTO on conveyor drives and motors
Grinding media charging in ball mills
Forklift operation in warehouse and packing areas

site_specific_hazard_awareness.timing: Before the person goes to the mine site
minimum_content: Site-specific hazards (mobile equipment, highwalls, process areas, traffic patterns)
PPE requirements for the areas to be visited

training_plan_requirements.approval: Must be approved by MSHA District Manager upon request
required_contents: Mine name, MSHA mine ID, and operator name
Name and position of person responsible for health and safety training
General description of teaching methods and course materials
Approximate duration of each type of training
List of competent persons who will provide training
training_plan_requirements.availability: Must be available at the mine site for inspection by MSHA and for examination by miners and their representatives
update_triggers: Change in mine operations that affect training needs
Change in competent persons delivering training

record_keeping.retention_note: Records for currently employed miners must be kept for duration of employment plus 3 years
required_fields: Type of training (new miner, experienced miner, refresher, task, hazard awareness)
Description of training content and materials used
Name of competent person who provided training
Evaluation of trainee competency (method and result)
Signature of competent person or documentation of electronic verification
record_keeping.format: Paper or electronic records are acceptable; must be readily accessible for MSHA inspection
common_deficiencies: Missing task training records for specific equipment
Training records not available at the mine site during inspection

competent_person_requirement.definition: A person designated by the production-operator or independent contractor who has the ability, training, knowledge, or experience to provide training to miners in his or her area of expertise
qualifications: Must be able to effectively communicate the training content
Must have knowledge and experience in the subject matter
Does not require formal instructor certification
May be an employee of the mine operator or an outside trainer
competent_person_requirement.documentation: Competent persons must be listed in the training plan with their areas of expertise
cement_plant_examples: Plant safety manager for general safety topics
Maintenance supervisor for LOTO and equipment-specific training
Quarry foreman for mobile equipment and blasting procedures
Process engineer for kiln and mill operation safety
Certified first aid/CPR instructor for first aid training
Outside vendor trainer for specialized equipment
